current_question: Does the current ACTIVE annual anti-edge boundary remain strong enough to keep ACTIVE when pressure-tested against grouped support, mixed blocked-cohort decomposition, exact-surface D1 evidence, and first external falsification?

in: grouped annual proxy support for blocked baseline-long quality inversion
blocked-cohort decomposition into displacement and suppressor carriers
exact-surface insufficient_evidence D1 evidence only as a narrowing carrier surface
external falsifier evidence that limits transport and portability
fixed local 2024 harmful-pocket discriminator only as challenge-context evidence
out: claiming one narrow carrier is the full explanation
editing previously modified edge_topology phase artifacts

narrowing_surfaces.role: mixed blocked-surface decomposition; insufficient_evidence sharper than aged_weak among true suppressors
exact 2024 local blocked-target split; local weakness carried more by insufficient_evidence subset
re-anchored exact-surface D1 family on action_edge/confidence_gate/clarity_raw
first exact harmful-vs-control subject pair evidence

challenge_surfaces.role: first fresh external falsifier; unchanged transport fails
local harmful-pocket shell may be broader than one narrow insufficient_evidence carrier
rejection of shared-pocket-presence as the main discriminator

working_hypotheses.claim: The ACTIVE label survives at the grouped annual proxy level, but must be explicitly narrowed away from portable carrier claims.
The blocked action-layer boundary is real, but carrier explanations should stay WEAK because the mixed blocked surface and external falsifier limit transport.
The total evidence now undercuts the ACTIVE label enough that the zone should be downgraded to WEAK.
